era: 1984-1997. Heavy Metal. E=9 -- sustained vocal and guitar intensity at maximum. U-District, Seattle. A&M Records.
note_cornell: Chris Cornell (July 20, 1964 - May 18, 2017). Died by suicide in Detroit following Soundgarden concert. Remembered here with full respect. His creative legacy -- Soundgarden, Temple of the Dog, Audioslave, solo -- is one of the most complete in American rock.

theory_nodes.concept: Cornell vocal range: four octaves, baritone to falsetto. The falsetto is structural, not decorative -- moments of release after mid-range pressure. Physical intensity of full-voice singing with actual cost.
theory_nodes.description: The widest-range and most physically committed vocal in the grunge era.

theory_nodes.concept: Thayil guitar: Eastern modal influence (South Asian-American background), non-standard tunings, feedback structural use, counter-rhythms against vocal. Not standard metal guitar -- a specific synthesis.
theory_nodes.description: Eastern modal influence + Western heavy metal: the specific Soundgarden sound.

theory_nodes.concept: Superunknown (1994): 79 min, 15 tracks, No. 1 Billboard, 4x platinum, two Grammys. The most complete artistic statement of the grunge era. Black Hole Sun chord progression G-Bb-F-Eb-A is immediately identifiable. Spoonman with Artis the Spoonman street musician.
theory_nodes.description: The grunge era's most ambitious and commercially successful heavy album.

theory_nodes.concept: Metal Summit position 3 of 4. E=9 through sustained vocal-guitar intensity. Mechanism: sustained maximum intensity across full album scope, not subsonic weight (Melvins) or dark synthesis (AIC).
theory_nodes.description: The Heavy Metal pillar of the Metal Summit.

cross_references.relationship: Metal Summit positions 2 and 3. Both E=9. Contemporaneous on major labels 1993-1994. Houdini and Superunknown released in same window. Different approaches: sludge weight (Melvins) vs. sustained intensity (Soundgarden).
Metal Summit continues. Both E=9. Both Seattle. Both active 1990-1994 at commercial peak. Cornell died 2017; Staley 2002. Both handled respectfully.
E=9 + E=9 = E=18. Sustained ultra-intensity howl (SPS) pairs with sustained vocal-guitar intensity (S36). Both are defined by sustained maximum at a single overwhelming register.

milestones: SUPERUNKNOWN -- THE GRUNGE ERA'S MOST AMBITIOUS ALBUM
